turns.content: What were the Papal Bulls "Dum Diversas" and "Romanus Pontifex", and what were their primary purposes?

The Papal Bulls "Dum Diversas" and "Romanus Pontifex" were two significant documents issued by the Catholic Church in the 15th century. 

"Dum Diversas" was a papal bull issued by Pope Nicholas V on June 18, 1452. The primary purpose of this bull was to grant the Portuguese monarchs, Afonso V and his mother, Queen Eleanor, the right to conquer and enslave non-Christians, particularly in West Africa.
